pattern: The minimum prefix to drop is exactly the length of the array minus its longest strictly increasing suffix, found with one backward scan.
hints: Removing the smallest possible prefix is the same as keeping the largest possible suffix - which suffixes are valid to keep?
Scan from the end: how far left can you extend a strictly increasing run before two adjacent elements break the order?
Walk backward from the last index while nums[i-1] < nums[i]; the first index where that fails (or index 0) is the length of the removed prefix.

edge_inputs.description: strictly decreasing array: keep only the last element
two equal elements are not strictly increasing
a duplicate at the very end breaks the suffix
negative numbers with a duplicate in the middle of the suffix

stress.description: large array with a random-length increasing tail appended to noisy data
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    tail_len = rng.randint(1, n)
    head_len = n - tail_len
    tail_start = rng.randint(-10**9, 10**9 - tail_len)
    tail = list(range(tail_start, tail_start + tail_len))
    head = [rng.randint(-10**9, 10**9) for _ in range(head_len)]
    if head:
        head[-1] = tail[0]  # force the head/tail boundary to break strict increase
    return [head + tail]
